MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library
  • Examples
  • Release Notes
LowLatency C++

It demonstrates how to use Magewell APIs to:

  1. preview videos from 2 different channels simultaneously, one in regular mode, and the other in low-latency mode. However, preview the same one video in 2 different modes are not supported using Pro Capture Family Functions.

  2. display capture latency and process delay in real time. This example is useful for users who want to know more about the latency.

    The main features of example are as follows:

    1. Start a standard video capture thread, and compress the captured frames into JPEG using Pro Capture Family Functions.
    2. Start a low latency video capture thread, and compress the captured frames into JPEG using Pro Capture Family Functions.
    3. Capture and render a data stream using Pro Capture Family Functions with timer CaptureByTimer C++.

    Only one capture mode can be selected at a time. For scenarioes with strict real-time requirements, you can refer to the low-latency mode of this exapmle.

Folder dir: SDKv3\Examples\VC++\GUI\LowLatency

Supported Hardware

Pro Capture Family

Features

  1. Support optional devices.
  2. Capture video in normal and low latency mode, and compress the captured frames into JPEG figures.
  3. Preview captured videos.

Workflow

  1. To initialize and enumerate all available devices, call: MWCaptureInitInStance, MWGetChannelCount
  2. To find the valid capture card, call: MWGetChannelInfoByIndex
  3. To start a standard video capture thread, and compress the captured frames into JPEG.
  4. To start a low latency video capture thread, and compress the captured frames into JPEG.
  5. To start a preview thread.
  6. To close the capture channel, release system resources, call: MWCaptureExitInstance

Flow Chart

Normal Mode
Low Latency Mode
Render and Capture Thread

Result

Low Latency
